scope.summary: Implement 3D-sketch-to-pattern tool: accept a polyline/spline drawn on or around the avatar mesh surface (3D Pen mode), project it via SDF closest-point, and flatten the resulting 3D curve to a GarmentSpec Panel outline using ARAP-consistent unfolding
acceptance_criteria: sketch_on_avatar(avatar_id, stroke_3d: Vec<[f32;3]>) projects the stroke onto the avatar SDF surface via closest-point query, returns a flattened 2D Panel outline in cm
The flattened Panel passes PANEL_CLOSED ValidationDescriptor and has seam-compatible edge lengths within 2% of the geodesic arc length on the avatar surface
The resulting Panel is inserted into tailor_panels via TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded
Model can supply a closed stroke to produce a single panel or an open stroke to produce a seam line on an existing panel
Flattening uses the same ARAP solver invoked by UV-from-pattern (MT-213) so the two paths are consistent
scope.risk_if_missed: Without 3D Pen / 3D Pencil, designers cannot sketch garment shapes directly on the body — they must work entirely in abstract 2D coordinate space, a major MD workflow advantage Tailor fails to replicate. The sketch-to-pattern entry point is the fastest path for bespoke garment authoring and is also the primary input for LLM-driven pattern generation from image references (Dress-1-to-3, ChatGarment downstream paths).

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
